qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SQL injection via unescaped quote character in quoteIdentifier() through filter object keys
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets Platform.quoteIdentifier(), CriteriaNode dotted-key handling, and identifier quoting for filter object keys. The advisory targets duck-typed internal ORM marker properties that let crafted objects be interpreted as raw SQL fragments in write APIs such as wrap(entity).assign(), nativeUpdate(), nativeInsert(), and create() followed by flush(). This is the same broad CWE class but a different vulnerable code path and root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No generated qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The only final qualifying finding is a SQL injection through unescaped quote characters in identifier quoting for dotted filter keys, while GHSA-gwhv-j974-6fxm describes crafted objects being mistaken for internal raw SQL fragments because ORM marker properties were duck-typed instead of symbol-based.
evidence: Advisory details state the affected usage is wrap(entity).assign(userInput), em.nativeUpdate(), em.nativeInsert(), and em.create() followed by flush(), with the fix replacing duck-typed internal marker checks with symbol-based markers.

sql-injection-quoteidentifier/vulnerability_report.json describes Platform.quoteIdentifier(), CriteriaNode, ObjectCriteriaNode, and QueryBuilderHelper paths for dotted filter keys containing quote characters.
.judge/input_manifest.json lists sql-injection-quoteidentifier as the only final qualifying vulnerability folder.
blockers_to_higher_tier: Tiering is not applicable because there is no advisory-matched qualifying finding.
